# 1

write a markdown to html converter in ts. dont use libraries.

---

Here is a complete, standalone TypeScript implementation of a Markdown to HTML converter. It handles the most common Markdown syntax (headings, lists, links, images, code blocks, bold/italic, and blockquotes) without using any external libraries.

````typescript
/**
 * Helper to escape HTML special characters to prevent XSS.
 */
function escapeHtml(text: string): string {
  return text
    .replace(/&/g, "&amp;")
    .replace(/</g, "&lt;")
    .replace(/>/g, "&gt;")
    .replace(/"/g, "&quot;")
    .replace(/'/g, "&#039;");
}

/**
 * Parses inline Markdown elements (bold, italic, links, images, code).
 * Order matters: Code/Images first, then Links, then formatting.
 */
function parseInline(text: string): string {
  // 1. Escape HTML first for safety
  text = escapeHtml(text);

  // 2. Inline Code (`code`)
  // Note: We remove the backticks here.
  text = text.replace(/`([^`]+)`/g, "<code>$1</code>");

  // 3. Images (![alt](url)) - Must be before links
  text = text.replace(/!\[([^\]]+)\]\(([^)]+)\)/g, '<img alt="$1" src="$2">');

  // 4. Links ([text](url))
  text = text.replace(/\[([^\]]+)\]\(([^)]+)\)/g, '<a href="$2">$1</a>');

  // 5. Bold (**text** or __text__)
  // Using (.+?) for non-greedy match to handle cases like **bold *italic*** (mostly)
  text = text.replace(/\*\*(.+?)\*\*/g, "<strong>$1</strong>");
  text = text.replace(/__(.+?)__/g, "<strong>$1</strong>");

  // 6. Italic (*text* or _text_)
  text = text.replace(/\*(.+?)\*/g, "<em>$1</em>");
  text = text.replace(/_(.+?)_/g, "<em>$1</em>");

  return text;
}

/**
 * Main Converter Function
 */
export function markdownToHtml(markdown: string): string {
  const lines = markdown.split("\n");
  let html = "";

  // State tracking
  let inCodeBlock = false;
  let codeLanguage = "";
  let codeContent = "";

  // List State
  let currentList: "ul" | "ol" | null = null;
  let listItems: string[] = [];

  // Helper to flush list buffer to HTML
  const flushList = () => {
    if (listItems.length > 0 && currentList) {
      html += `<${currentList}>`;
      for (const item of listItems) {
        html += `<li>${item}</li>`;
      }
      html += `</${currentList}>`;
      listItems = [];
      currentList = null;
    }
  };

  // Iterate through every line
  for (let i = 0; i < lines.length; i++) {
    let line = lines[i];

    // --- 1. Code Blocks (Fenced) ---
    if (line.trim().startsWith("```")) {
      if (inCodeBlock) {
        // End of code block
        html += `<pre><code class="language-${codeLanguage}">${escapeHtml(
          codeContent
        )}</code></pre>\n`;
        codeContent = "";
        inCodeBlock = false;
        codeLanguage = "";
      } else {
        // Start of code block
        flushList();
        inCodeBlock = true;
        // Extract language (e.g., ```js)
        codeLanguage = line.trim().slice(3);
      }
      continue;
    }

    if (inCodeBlock) {
      // Accumulate content without processing inline markdown
      codeContent += line + "\n";
      continue;
    }

    // --- 2. Blockquotes (Lines starting with >) ---
    const blockquoteMatch = line.match(/^>\s?(.*)/);
    if (blockquoteMatch) {
      flushList();
      const text = parseInline(blockquoteMatch[1]);

      // Check if the previous output ended with a blockquote to merge lines
      if (html.endsWith("</blockquote>\n")) {
        // Remove closing tag, add break, add text, re-add closing tag
        html = html.slice(0, -13) + `<br>${text}</blockquote>\n`;
      } else {
        html += `<blockquote>${text}</blockquote>\n`;
      }
      continue;
    }

    // --- 3. Headings (# Header) ---
    const headingMatch = line.match(/^(#{1,6})\s+(.*)/);
    if (headingMatch) {
      flushList();
      const level = headingMatch[1].length;
      const text = parseInline(headingMatch[2]);
      html += `<h${level}>${text}</h${level}>\n`;
      continue;
    }

    // --- 4. Lists (- Item or 1. Item) ---
    // Unordered
    const unorderedMatch = line.match(/^[\*\-\+]\s+(.*)/);
    // Ordered
    const orderedMatch = line.match(/^\d+\.\s+(.*)/);

    if (unorderedMatch) {
      if (currentList !== "ul") {
        flushList();
        currentList = "ul";
      }
      listItems.push(parseInline(unorderedMatch[1]));
      continue;
    } else if (orderedMatch) {
      if (currentList !== "ol") {
        flushList();
        currentList = "ol";
      }
      listItems.push(parseInline(orderedMatch[1]));
      continue;
    }

    // --- 5. Standard Paragraph ---
    flushList(); // Ensure lists are closed before a new paragraph

    if (line.trim() === "") {
      continue; // Skip empty lines
    }

    html += `<p>${parseInline(line)}</p>\n`;
  }

  // Handle edge case: Document ends while inside a list or code block
  flushList();
  if (inCodeBlock) {
    html += `<pre><code class="language-${codeLanguage}">${escapeHtml(
      codeContent
    )}</code></pre>`;
  }

  return html;
}
````

### How to use it

1.  **Save**: Save the code above as `markdown.ts`.
2.  **Run**: You can compile this with `tsc markdown.ts` and run it with node, or simply copy the function into your TS/JS project.

### Example Input/Output

**Input:**

```markdown
# Hello World

This is a paragraph with **bold** and _italic_ text.

- Item 1
- Item 2

> This is a blockquote
> spanning two lines.

Check out this [link](https://example.com).
```

**Output:**

```html
<h1>Hello World</h1>
<p>This is a paragraph with <strong>bold</strong> and <em>italic</em> text.</p>
<ul>
  <li>Item 1</li>
  <li>Item 2</li>
</ul>
<blockquote>This is a blockquote<br />spanning two lines.</blockquote>
<p>Check out this <a href="https://example.com">link</a>.</p>
```

Let's use a regex that captures the fence and content.&nbsp;<code data-streamdown="inline-code" class="rounded bg-muted px-1.5 py-0.5 font-mono text-sm" style="--tw-border-spacing-x: 0; --tw-border-spacing-y: 0; --tw-translate-x: 0; --tw-translate-y: 0; --tw-rotate: 0; --tw-skew-x: 0; --tw-skew-y: 0; --tw-scale-x: 1; --tw-scale-y: 1; --tw-pan-x: ; --tw-pan-y: ; --tw-pinch-zoom: ; --tw-scroll-snap-strictness: proximity; --tw-gradient-from-position: ; --tw-gradient-via-position: ; --tw-gradient-to-position: ; --tw-ordinal: ; --tw-slashed-zero: ; --tw-numeric-figure: ; --tw-numeric-spacing: ; --tw-numeric-fraction: ; --tw-ring-inset: ; --tw-ring-offset-width: 0px; --tw-ring-offset-color: #fff; --tw-ring-color: rgba(59,130,246,.5); --tw-ring-offset-shadow: 0 0 #0000; --tw-ring-shadow: 0 0 #0000; --tw-shadow: 0 0 #0000; --tw-shadow-colored: 0 0 #0000; --tw-blur: ; --tw-brightness: ; --tw-contrast: ; --tw-grayscale: ; --tw-hue-rotate: ; --tw-invert: ; --tw-saturate: ; --tw-sepia: ; --tw-drop-shadow: ; --tw-backdrop-blur: ; --tw-backdrop-brightness: ; --tw-backdrop-contrast: ; --tw-backdrop-grayscale: ; --tw-backdrop-hue-rotate: ; --tw-backdrop-invert: ; --tw-backdrop-opacity: ; --tw-backdrop-saturate: ; --tw-backdrop-sepia: ; --tw-contain-size: ; --tw-contain-layout: ; --tw-contain-paint: ; --tw-contain-style: ; box-sizing: border-box; border: 0px solid rgb(229, 231, 235); font-family: ui-monospace, SFMono-Regular, Menlo, Monaco, Consolas, &quot;Liberation Mono&quot;, &quot;Courier New&quot;, monospace; font-feature-settings: normal; font-variation-settings: normal; font-size: 0.875rem; border-radius: 0.25rem; background-color: var(--muted); padding: 0.125rem 0.375rem; line-height: 1.25rem;">const codeBlockRegex = /```(\w*)\n([\s\S]*?)```/g;</code>&nbsp;This is easier than a state machine for a simple script. But what if there is text&nbsp;<em style="--tw-border-spacing-x: 0; --tw-border-spacing-y: 0; --tw-translate-x: 0; --tw-translate-y: 0; --tw-rotate: 0; --tw-skew-x: 0; --tw-skew-y: 0; --tw-scale-x: 1; --tw-scale-y: 1; --tw-pan-x: ; --tw-pan-y: ; --tw-pinch-zoom: ; --tw-scroll-snap-strictness: proximity; --tw-gradient-from-position: ; --tw-gradient-via-position: ; --tw-gradient-to-position: ; --tw-ordinal: ; --tw-slashed-zero: ; --tw-numeric-figure: ; --tw-numeric-spacing: ; --tw-numeric-fraction: ; --tw-ring-inset: ; --tw-ring-offset-width: 0px; --tw-ring-offset-color: #fff; --tw-ring-color: rgba(59,130,246,.5); --tw-ring-offset-shadow: 0 0 #0000; --tw-ring-shadow: 0 0 #0000; --tw-shadow: 0 0 #0000; --tw-shadow-colored: 0 0 #0000; --tw-blur: ; --tw-brightness: ; --tw-contrast: ; --tw-grayscale: ; --tw-hue-rotate: ; --tw-invert: ; --tw-saturate: ; --tw-sepia: ; --tw-drop-shadow: ; --tw-backdrop-blur: ; --tw-backdrop-brightness: ; --tw-backdrop-contrast: ; --tw-backdrop-grayscale: ; --tw-backdrop-hue-rotate: ; --tw-backdrop-invert: ; --tw-backdrop-opacity: ; --tw-backdrop-saturate: ; --tw-backdrop-sepia: ; --tw-contain-size: ; --tw-contain-layout: ; --tw-contain-paint: ; --tw-contain-style: ; box-sizing: border-box; border: 0px solid rgb(229, 231, 235);">between</em>&nbsp;code blocks? font-size: 0.875rem; line-height: 1.25rem; --tw-text-opacity: 1; color: rgb(185 28 28/var(--tw-text-opacity,1));">Mermaid Error:&nbsp;No diagram type detected matching given configuration for text: export function markdownToHtml(markdown: string): string { const lines = markdown.split('\n'); let html = ''; let inCodeBlock = false; let inList = false; let listType: 'ul' | 'ol' | null = null; let listBuffer: string[] = []; // Helper to flush list const flushList = () =&gt; { if (!listBuffer.length) return; html += `&lt;${listType}&gt;\n`; for (const item of listBuffer) { html += `&lt;li&gt;${item}&lt;/li&gt;\n`; } html += `&lt;/${listType}&gt;\n`; listBuffer = []; inList = false; listType = null; }; for (let i = 0; i &lt; lines.length; i++) { let line = lines[i]; // Code Blocks if (line.trim().startsWith('```')) { if (inCodeBlock) { html += '&lt;/code&gt;&lt;/pre&gt;\n'; inCodeBlock = false; continue; } else { flushList(); // Close lists before code block const lang = line.trim().slice(3); html += `&lt;pre&gt;&lt;code class='language-${lang}'&gt;`; inCodeBlock = true; continue; } } if (inCodeBlock) { html += line + '\n'; continue; } // Headers const headerMatch = line.match(/^(#{1,6})\s+(.*)/); if (headerMatch) { flushList(); const level = headerMatch[1].length; const text = parseInline(headerMatch[2]); html += `&lt;h${level}&gt;${text}&lt;/h${level}&gt;\n`; continue; } // Blockquotes if (line.trim().startsWith('&gt;')) { flushList(); // Support multi-line blockquotes? Usually starts with &gt; on first line. // Let's treat every line starting with &gt; as part of the quote block for simplicity, // or wrap individually. Let's wrap individually or handle accumulation. // Let's keep it simple: simple line-based. const text = parseInline(line.replace(/^&gt;\s?/, '')); html += `&lt;blockquote&gt;${text}&lt;/blockquote&gt;\n`; continue; } // Lists const unorderedMatch = line.match(/^[\*\-\+]\s+(.*)/); const orderedMatch = line.match(/^\d+\.\s+(.*)/); if (unorderedMatch) { const text = parseInline(unorderedMatch[1]); if (listType === 'ul' &amp;&amp; inList) { listBuffer.push(text); } else { flushList(); listType = 'ul'; inList = true; listBuffer.push(text); } continue; } else if (orderedMatch) { const text = parseInline(orderedMatch[1]); if (listType === 'ol' &amp;&amp; inList) { listBuffer.push(text); } else { flushList(); listType = 'ol'; inList = true; listBuffer.push(text); } continue; } // Standard Paragraph flushList(); if (line.trim() === '') continue; // Skip empty lines html += `&lt;p&gt;${parseInline(line)}&lt;/p&gt;\n`; } flushList(); // Flush remaining list return html; } ```*$</p><details class="mt-2" style="--tw-border-spacing-x: 0; --tw-border-spacing-y: 0; --tw-translate-x: 0; --tw-translate-y: 0; --tw-rotate: 0; Usually, you press space at start of next line. But standard markdown is line-based. I'll assume one line = one item for simplicity, unless I add complex logic. Given "dont use libraries", line-based is acceptable.</p><p style="--tw-border-spacing-x: 0; --tw-border-spacing-y: 0; --tw-translate-x: 0; --tw-translate-y: 0; --tw-rotate: 0; --tw-skew-x: 0; --tw-skew-y: 0; Correct HTML for multi-line quote is usually one blockquote with newlines or&nbsp;<code data-streamdown="inline-code" class="rounded bg-muted px-1.5 py-0.5 font-mono text-sm" style="--tw-border-spacing-x: 0; --tw-border-spacing-y: 0; --tw-translate-x: 0; --tw-translate-y: 0; --tw-rotate: 0; --tw-skew-x: 0; --tw-skew-y: 0; --tw-scale-x: 1; --tw-scale-y: 1; --tw-pan-x: ; --tw-pan-y: ; --tw-pinch-zoom: ; --tw-scroll-snap-strictness: proximity; --tw-gradient-from-position: ; --tw-gradient-via-position: ; --tw-gradient-to-position: ; --tw-ordinal: ; --tw-slashed-zero: ; --tw-numeric-figure: ; --tw-numeric-spacing: ; --tw-numeric-fraction: ; --tw-ring-inset: ; --tw-ring-offset-width: 0px; --tw-ring-offset-color: #fff; --tw-ring-color: rgba(59,130,246,.5); --tw-ring-offset-shadow: 0 0 #0000; --tw-ring-shadow: 0 0 #0000; --tw-shadow: 0 0 #0000; --tw-shadow-colored: 0 0 #0000; --tw-blur: ; --tw-brightness: ; --tw-contrast: ; --tw-grayscale: ; --tw-hue-rotate: ; --tw-invert: ; --tw-saturate: ; --tw-sepia: ; --tw-drop-shadow: ; --tw-backdrop-blur: ; --tw-backdrop-brightness: ; --tw-backdrop-contrast: ; --tw-backdrop-grayscale: ; --tw-backdrop-hue-rotate: ; --tw-backdrop-invert: ; --tw-backdrop-opacity: ; --tw-backdrop-saturate: ; --tw-backdrop-sepia: ; --tw-contain-size: ; --tw-contain-layout: ; --tw-contain-paint: ; --tw-contain-style: ; box-sizing: border-box; border: 0px solid rgb(229, 231, 235); font-family: ui-monospace, SFMono-Regular, Menlo, Monaco, Consolas, &quot;Liberation Mono&quot;, &quot;Courier New&quot;, monospace; font-feature-settings: normal; font-variation-settings: normal; font-size: 0.875rem; border-radius: 0.25rem; background-color: var(--muted); padding: 0.125rem 0.375rem; line-height: 1.25rem;">&lt;br&gt;</code>. To fix: Check if previous line was blockquote. If so, append to previous blockquote instead of creating new. I'll implement a simple check:&nbsp;<code data-streamdown="inline-code" class="rounded bg-muted px-1.5 py-0.5 font-mono text-sm" style="--tw-border-spacing-x: 0; --tw-border-spacing-y: 0; --tw-translate-x: 0; --tw-translate-y: 0; --tw-rotate: 0; --tw-skew-x: 0; --tw-skew-y: 0; --tw-scale-x: 1; --tw-scale-y: 1; --tw-pan-x: ; --tw-pan-y: ; --tw-pinch-zoom: ; --tw-scroll-snap-strictness: proximity; --tw-gradient-from-position: ; --tw-gradient-via-position: ; --tw-gradient-to-position: ; --tw-ordinal: ; --tw-slashed-zero: ; --tw-numeric-figure: ; --tw-numeric-spacing: ; --tw-numeric-fraction: ; --tw-ring-inset: ; --tw-ring-offset-width: 0px; --tw-ring-offset-color: #fff; --tw-ring-color: rgba(59,130,246,.5); --tw-ring-offset-shadow: 0 0 #0000; --tw-ring-shadow: 0 0 #0000; --tw-shadow: 0 0 #0000; --tw-shadow-colored: 0 0 #0000; --tw-blur: ; --tw-brightness: ; --tw-contrast: ; --tw-grayscale: ; --tw-hue-rotate: ; --tw-invert: ; --tw-saturate: ; --tw-sepia: ; --tw-drop-shadow: ; --tw-backdrop-blur: ; --tw-backdrop-brightness: ; --tw-backdrop-contrast: ; --tw-backdrop-grayscale: ; --tw-backdrop-hue-rotate: ; --tw-backdrop-invert: ; --tw-backdrop-opacity: ; --tw-backdrop-saturate: ; --tw-backdrop-sepia: ; --tw-contain-size: ; --tw-contain-layout: ; --tw-contain-paint: ; --tw-contain-style: ; box-sizing: border-box; border: 0px solid rgb(229, 231, 235); font-family: ui-monospace, SFMono-Regular, Menlo, Monaco, Consolas, &quot;Liberation Mono&quot;, &quot;Courier New&quot;, monospace; font-feature-settings: normal; font-variation-settings: normal; font-size: 0.875rem; border-radius: 0.25rem; background-color: var(--muted); padding: 0.125rem 0.375rem; line-height: 1.25rem;">let lastTagWasBlockquote = false;</code>&nbsp;If I output blockquote, set flag. Next iteration, if blockquote, append to previous (remove closing tag, add content, add open tag? or just&nbsp;<code data-streamdown="inline-code" class="rounded bg-muted px-1.5 py-0.5 font-mono text-sm" style="--tw-border-spacing-x: 0; --tw-border-spacing-y: 0; --tw-translate-x: 0; --tw-translate-y: 0; --tw-rotate: 0; --tw-skew-x: 0; --tw-skew-y: 0; --tw-scale-x: 1; --tw-scale-y: 1; --tw-pan-x: ; --tw-pan-y: ; --tw-pinch-zoom: ; --tw-scroll-snap-strictness: proximity; --tw-gradient-from-position: ; --tw-gradient-via-position: ; --tw-gradient-to-position: ; --tw-ordinal: ; --tw-slashed-zero: ; --tw-numeric-figure: ; --tw-numeric-spacing: ; --tw-numeric-fraction: ; --tw-ring-inset: ; --tw-ring-offset-width: 0px; --tw-ring-offset-color: #fff; --tw-ring-color: rgba(59,130,246,.5); --tw-ring-offset-shadow: 0 0 #0000; --tw-ring-shadow: 0 0 #0000; --tw-shadow: 0 0 #0000; --tw-shadow-colored: 0 0 #0000; --tw-blur: ; --tw-brightness: ; --tw-contrast: ; --tw-grayscale: ; --tw-hue-rotate: ; --tw-invert: ; --tw-saturate: ; --tw-sepia: ; --tw-drop-shadow: ; --tw-backdrop-blur: ; --tw-backdrop-brightness: ; --tw-backdrop-contrast: ; --tw-backdrop-grayscale: ; --tw-backdrop-hue-rotate: ; --tw-backdrop-invert: ; --tw-backdrop-opacity: ; --tw-backdrop-saturate: ; --tw-backdrop-sepia: ; --tw-contain-size: ; --tw-contain-layout: ; --tw-contain-paint: ; --tw-contain-style: ; box-sizing: border-box; border: 0px solid rgb(229, 231, 235); font-family: ui-monospace, SFMono-Regular, Menlo, Monaco, Consolas, &quot;Liberation Mono&quot;, &quot;Courier New&quot;, monospace; font-feature-settings: normal; font-variation-settings: normal; font-size: 0.875rem; border-radius: 0.25rem; background-color: var(--muted); padding: 0.125rem 0.375rem; line-height: 1.25rem;">&lt;br&gt;</code>). To properly support nesting, you need a parser. Given "no libraries" and "basic converter", I will assume the user won't do&nbsp;<code data-streamdown="inline-code" class="rounded bg-muted px-1.5 py-0.5 font-mono text-sm" style="--tw-border-spacing-x: 0; --tw-border-spacing-y: 0; --tw-translate-x: 0; --tw-translate-y: 0; --tw-rotate: 0; --tw-skew-x: 0; --tw-skew-y: 0; --tw-scale-x: 1; --tw-scale-y: 1; --tw-pan-x: ; --tw-pan-y: ; --tw-pinch-zoom: ; --tw-scroll-snap-strictness: proximity; --tw-gradient-from-position: ; --tw-gradient-via-position: ; --tw-gradient-to-position: ; --tw-ordinal: ; --tw-slashed-zero: ; --tw-numeric-figure: ; --tw-numeric-spacing: ; --tw-numeric-fraction: ; --tw-ring-inset: ; --tw-ring-offset-width: 0px; --tw-ring-offset-color: #fff; --tw-ring-color: rgba(59,130,246,.5); --tw-ring-offset-shadow: 0 0 #0000; --tw-ring-shadow: 0 0 #0000; --tw-shadow: 0 0 #0000; --tw-shadow-colored: 0 0 #0000; --tw-blur: ; --tw-brightness: ; --tw-contrast: ; --tw-grayscale: ; --tw-hue-rotate: ; --tw-invert: ; --tw-saturate: ; --tw-sepia: ; --tw-drop-shadow: ; --tw-backdrop-blur: ; --tw-backdrop-brightness: ; --tw-backdrop-contrast: ; --tw-backdrop-grayscale: ; --tw-backdrop-hue-rotate: ; --tw-backdrop-invert: ; --tw-backdrop-opacity: ; --tw-backdrop-saturate: ; --tw-backdrop-sepia: ; --tw-contain-size: ; --tw-contain-layout: ; --tw-contain-paint: ; --tw-contain-style: ; box-sizing: border-box; border: 0px solid rgb(229, 231, 235); font-family: ui-monospace, SFMono-Regular, Menlo, Monaco, Consolas, &quot;Liberation Mono&quot;, &quot;Courier New&quot;, monospace; font-feature-settings: normal; font-variation-settings: normal; font-size: 0.875rem; border-radius: 0.25rem; background-color: var(--muted); padding: 0.125rem 0.375rem; line-height: 1.25rem;">**bold *italic**</code>&nbsp;(overlapping delimiters). "Link " is left over. --tw-backdrop-contrast: ; --tw-backdrop-grayscale: ; --tw-backdrop-hue-rotate: ; --tw-backdrop-invert: ; --tw-backdrop-opacity: ; --tw-backdrop-saturate: ; --tw-backdrop-sepia: ; --tw-contain-size: ; --tw-contain-layout: ; --tw-contain-paint: ; --tw-contain-style: ; box-sizing: border-box; border: 0px solid rgb(229, 231, 235); margin: 0px; font-family: ui-monospace, SFMono-Regular, Menlo, Monaco, Consolas, &quot;Liberation Mono&quot;, &quot;Courier New&quot;, monospace; font-size: 0.875rem; line-height: 1.25rem; --tw-text-opacity: 1; color: rgb(185 28 28/var(--tw-text-opacity,1));">Mermaid Error:&nbsp;No diagram type detected matching given configuration for text: /** * Helper to escape HTML special characters to prevent XSS. */ function escapeHtml(text: string): string { return text .replace(/&amp;/g, "&amp;amp;") .replace(/&lt;/g, "&amp;lt;") .replace(/&gt;/g, "&amp;gt;") .replace(/"/g, "&amp;quot;") .replace(/'/g, "&amp;#039;"); } /** * Parses inline Markdown elements (bold, italic, links, images, code). * Order matters: Code/Images first, then Links, then formatting. */ function parseInline(text: string): string { // 1. Escape HTML first for safety text = escapeHtml(text); // 2. Inline Code (`code`) // Note: We remove the backticks here. text = text.replace(/`([^`]+)`/g, '&lt;code&gt;$1&lt;/code&gt;'); // 3. Images (![alt](url)) - Must be before links text = text.replace(/!\[([^\]]+)\]\(([^)]+)\)/g, '&lt;img alt='$1' src='$2'&gt;'); // 4. Links ([text](url)) text = text.replace(/\[([^\]]+)\]\(([^)]+)\)/g, '&lt;a href='$2'&gt;$1&lt;/a&gt;'); // 5. Bold (**text** or __text__) // Using (.+?) for non-greedy match to handle cases like **bold *italic*** (mostly) text = text.replace(/\*\*(.+?)\*\*/g, '&lt;strong&gt;$1&lt;/strong&gt;'); text = text.replace(/__(.+?)__/g, '&lt;strong&gt;$1&lt;/strong&gt;'); // 6. Italic (*text* or _text_) text = text.replace(/\*(.+?)\*/g, '&lt;em&gt;$1&lt;/em&gt;'); text = text.replace(/_(.+?)_/g, '&lt;em&gt;$1&lt;/em&gt;'); return text; } /** * Main Converter Function */ export function markdownToHtml(markdown: string): string { const lines = markdown.split('\n'); let html = ''; // State tracking let inCodeBlock = false; let codeLanguage = ''; let codeContent = ''; // List State let currentList: 'ul' | 'ol' | null = null; let listItems: string[] = []; // Helper to flush list buffer to HTML const flushList = () =&gt; { if (listItems.length &gt; 0 &amp;&amp; currentList) { html += `&lt;${currentList}&gt;`; for (const item of listItems) { html += `&lt;li&gt;${item}&lt;/li&gt;`; } html += `&lt;/${currentList}&gt;`; listItems = []; currentList = null; } }; // Iterate through every line for (let i = 0; i &lt; lines.length; i++) { let line = lines[i]; // --- 1. Code Blocks (Fenced) --- if (line.trim().startsWith('```')) { if (inCodeBlock) { // End of code block html += `&lt;pre&gt;&lt;code class='language-${codeLanguage}'&gt;${escapeHtml(codeContent)}&lt;/code&gt;&lt;/pre&gt;\n`; codeContent = ''; inCodeBlock = false; codeLanguage = ''; } else { // Start of code block flushList(); inCodeBlock = true; // Extract language (e.g., ```js) codeLanguage = line.trim().slice(3); } continue; } if (inCodeBlock) { // Accumulate content without processing inline markdown codeContent += line + '\n'; continue; } // --- 2. Blockquotes (Lines starting with &gt;) --- const blockquoteMatch = line.match(/^&gt;\s?(.*)/); if (blockquoteMatch) { flushList(); const text = parseInline(blockquoteMatch[1]); // Check if the previous output ended with a blockquote to merge lines if (html.endsWith('&lt;/blockquote&gt;\n')) { // Remove closing tag, add break, add text, re-add closing tag html = html.slice(0, -13) + `&lt;br&gt;${text}&lt;/blockquote&gt;\n`; } else { html += `&lt;blockquote&gt;${text}&lt;/blockquote&gt;\n`; } continue; } // --- 3. Headings (# Header) --- const headingMatch = line.match(/^(#{1,6})\s+(.*)/); if (headingMatch) { flushList(); const level = headingMatch[1].length; const text = parseInline(headingMatch[2]); html += `&lt;h${level}&gt;${text}&lt;/h${level}&gt;\n`; continue; } // --- 4. Lists (- Item or 1. Item) --- // Unordered const unorderedMatch = line.match(/^[\*\-\+]\s+(.*)/); // Ordered const orderedMatch = line.match(/^\d+\.\s+(.*)/); if (unorderedMatch) { if (currentList !== 'ul') { flushList(); currentList = 'ul'; } listItems.push(parseInline(unorderedMatch[1])); continue; } else if (orderedMatch) { if (currentList !== 'ol') { flushList(); currentList = 'ol'; } listItems.push(parseInline(orderedMatch[1])); continue; } // --- 5. Standard Paragraph --- flushList(); // Ensure lists are closed before a new paragraph if (line.trim() === '') { continue; // Skip empty lines } html += `&lt;p&gt;${parseInline(line)}&lt;/p&gt;\n`; } // Handle edge case: Document ends while inside a list or code block flushList(); if (inCodeBlock) { html += `&lt;pre&gt;&lt;code class='language-${codeLanguage}'&gt;${escapeHtml(codeContent)}&lt;/code&gt;&lt;/pre&gt;`; } return html; }</p><details class="mt-2" style="--tw-border-spacing-x: 0;
